title: Mixer Dominance Pilot v2

project_positioning.research_question: When does a more sophisticated classical optimizer justify its runtime, shot, queue, and mitigation cost for constrained QAOA portfolio optimization?
project_positioning.what_this_project_is: A hardware-aware benchmark platform for constrained QAOA portfolio optimization rather than a one-off demo or toy VQE script.
technical_contributions: Multi-regime synthetic portfolio generator with exact feasible references on small instances.
Constraint-aware QAOA benchmark with CVaR-style objectives, feasibility tracking, and valid-ratio histories.
Strict runtime accounting across objective calls, effective shots, transpilation time, execution time, queue latency, and estimated billed QPU cost.
Fair optimizer comparison across a classical Markowitz baseline, Random Search, SPSA, and Bayesian Optimization with periodic-safe surrogate handling for QAOA angles.
Per-instance QUBO structure diagnostics including constraint hardness and simple spectral-profile summaries.
project_positioning.why_it_is_interesting: It lets the user study when sophisticated classical search is actually worth its real hybrid-loop overhead, instead of assuming better optimization always means better practical performance.

classical_markowitz.approx_ratio_note: Ratio is normalized so >1 means worse than exact, but compare only within study slices whose exact-feasible reference sign is consistent.

random.approx_ratio_note: Ratio is normalized so >1 means worse than exact, but compare only within study slices whose exact-feasible reference sign is consistent.

spsa.approx_ratio_note: Ratio is normalized so >1 means worse than exact, but compare only within study slices whose exact-feasible reference sign is consistent.

bayesian_optimization.approx_ratio_note: Ratio is normalized so >1 means worse than exact, but compare only within study slices whose exact-feasible reference sign is consistent.

single_run_insights: On regime=baseline, Random Search achieved the best raw objective (-0.4940) relative to the exact feasible reference (-0.4940).
Feasibility was strongest for Classical Markowitz with feasible-hit rate 100.00% and best valid energy -0.4940.
Classical Markowitz was the cheapest quality-adjusted method once billed time and estimated QPU cost were included, highlighting the difference between best score and best practical tradeoff.
The BO path was configured with a feasibility-aware target, so the benchmark explicitly tests whether sophisticated search is learning portfolio quality or just surfing penalty walls.

spsa.approx_ratio_note: Ratio is normalized so >1 means worse than exact, but compare only within study slices whose exact-feasible reference sign is consistent.

bayesian_optimization.approx_ratio_note: Ratio is normalized so >1 means worse than exact, but compare only within study slices whose exact-feasible reference sign is consistent.

single_run_insights: On regime=baseline, Classical Markowitz achieved the best raw objective (-0.4281) relative to the exact feasible reference (-0.4281).
Feasibility was strongest for Classical Markowitz with feasible-hit rate 100.00% and best valid energy -0.4281.
The BO path was configured with a feasibility-aware target, so the benchmark explicitly tests whether sophisticated search is learning portfolio quality or just surfing penalty walls.

project_positioning.research_question: When does a more sophisticated classical optimizer justify its runtime, shot, queue, and mitigation cost for constrained QAOA portfolio optimization?
project_positioning.what_this_project_is: A hardware-aware benchmark platform for constrained QAOA portfolio optimization rather than a one-off demo or toy VQE script.
technical_contributions: Multi-regime synthetic portfolio generator with exact feasible references on small instances.
Constraint-aware QAOA benchmark with CVaR-style objectives, feasibility tracking, and valid-ratio histories.
Strict runtime accounting across objective calls, effective shots, transpilation time, execution time, queue latency, and estimated billed QPU cost.
Fair optimizer comparison across a classical Markowitz baseline, Random Search, SPSA, and Bayesian Optimization with periodic-safe surrogate handling for QAOA angles.
Per-instance QUBO structure diagnostics including constraint hardness and simple spectral-profile summaries.
project_positioning.why_it_is_interesting: It lets the user study when sophisticated classical search is actually worth its real hybrid-loop overhead, instead of assuming better optimization always means better practical performance.

bayesian_optimization.approx_ratio_note: Ratio is normalized so >1 means worse than exact, but compare only within study slices whose exact-feasible reference sign is consistent.

single_run_insights: On regime=hard_budget, Classical Markowitz achieved the best raw objective (-0.4581) relative to the exact feasible reference (-0.4581).
Feasibility was strongest for Classical Markowitz with feasible-hit rate 100.00% and best valid energy -0.4581.
The BO path was configured with a feasibility-aware target, so the benchmark explicitly tests whether sophisticated search is learning portfolio quality or just surfing penalty walls.

Constraint-aware QAOA benchmark with CVaR-style objectives, feasibility tracking, and valid-ratio histories.
Strict runtime accounting across objective calls, effective shots, transpilation time, execution time, queue latency, and estimated billed QPU cost.
Fair optimizer comparison across a classical Markowitz baseline, Random Search, SPSA, and Bayesian Optimization with periodic-safe surrogate handling for QAOA angles.
Per-instance QUBO structure diagnostics including constraint hardness and simple spectral-profile summaries.
project_positioning.why_it_is_interesting: It lets the user study when sophisticated classical search is actually worth its real hybrid-loop overhead, instead of assuming better optimization always means better practical performance.

classical_markowitz.approx_ratio_note: Ratio is normalized so >1 means worse than exact, but compare only within study slices whose exact-feasible reference sign is consistent.

random.approx_ratio_note: Ratio is normalized so >1 means worse than exact, but compare only within study slices whose exact-feasible reference sign is consistent.

spsa.approx_ratio_note: Ratio is normalized so >1 means worse than exact, but compare only within study slices whose exact-feasible reference sign is consistent.

bayesian_optimization.approx_ratio_note: Ratio is normalized so >1 means worse than exact, but compare only within study slices whose exact-feasible reference sign is consistent.

single_run_insights: On regime=hard_budget, Classical Markowitz achieved the best raw objective (-0.4087) relative to the exact feasible reference (-0.4087).
Feasibility was strongest for Classical Markowitz with feasible-hit rate 100.00% and best valid energy -0.4087.
The BO path was configured with a feasibility-aware target, so the benchmark explicitly tests whether sophisticated search is learning portfolio quality or just surfing penalty walls.

claims: For factorial=regime=baseline | n_assets=12 | mixer_type=product_x | depolarizing_strength_2q=0.001, Classical Markowitz has the best mean raw objective (-0.4886, bootstrap 95% CI -0.5492–-0.4281).

For factorial=regime=baseline | n_assets=12 | mixer_type=product_x | depolarizing_strength_2q=0.001, feasibility favors Bayesian Optimization (100.00%) even though the raw-objective winner is Classical Markowitz.

research_summary.research_question: When does a more sophisticated classical optimizer justify its runtime, shot, queue, and mitigation cost for constrained QAOA portfolio optimization?
research_summary.headline_answer: Across the completed suite, Classical Markowitz delivered the strongest mean objective on factorial=regime=baseline | n_assets=6 | mixer_type=product_x | depolarizing_strength_2q=0.001, while Classical Markowitz provided the best cost-adjusted tradeoff on at least one study point.
contributions_demonstrated: Constraint-aware QAOA benchmarking across multiple synthetic portfolio regimes.
Fair optimizer comparison under shared runtime, shot, and billing accounting.
Structured exports that preserve both benchmark claims and per-evaluation evidence.
evidence_summary: Best mean raw objective: Classical Markowitz on factorial=regime=baseline | n_assets=6 | mixer_type=product_x | depolarizing_strength_2q=0.001 with -0.5069.
Strongest feasibility: Bayesian Optimization reached mean feasible-hit rate 100.00%.
Best cost-adjusted method: Classical Markowitz minimized estimated cost plus elapsed time most effectively.
research_summary.application_ready_summary: This benchmark demonstrates the ability to build a reproducible, hardware-aware research instrument that connects constrained QAOA physics, optimizer design, and runtime-cost accounting into a single comparative framework.
